en.extract:
The black-banded barbet (Psilopogon javensis) is a bird species in the Megalaimidae family.
It is endemic to Java and Bali.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is threatened by habitat loss.
They are known to attract ecotourists to Java.

The '''black-banded barbet''' ('''''Psilopogon javensis''''') is a [[bird]] species in the [[Megalaimidae]] family.
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Java]] and [[Bali]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

They are known to attract ecotourists to Java.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Nijman |first=Vincent |date=2023-05-14 |title=Tourists, selfies and coastal monitoring during COVID-19 |journal=Journal of Ecotourism |volume=22 |issue=4 |pages=578–584 |doi=10.1080/14724049.2023.2212143 |issn=1472-4049|doi-access=free |bibcode=2023JEcot..22..578N }}</ref>
